title: podman machine do not use a `tab` but instead `spaces` to list the VM machines
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

The format used to stdout the list of the podman machine do not use a `tab` but instead `spaces`.
The list of the machines displayed should respect the format defined and documented and add a tab between the columns result
```
podman machine list --format "{{.Name}}\t{{.VMType}}\t{{.Created}}\t{{.LastUp}}\t{{.CPUs}}\t{{.Memory}}\t{{.DiskSize}}" | awk -F '\t' '{ print $2 }'

podman machine list --format "{{.Name}}\t{{.VMType}}\t{{.Created}}\t{{.LastUp}}\t{{.CPUs}}\t{{.Memory}}\t{{.DiskSize}}" | awk -F '\t' '{ print $1 }'
macvm*      qemu        2 hours ago  About an hour ago  1           2.147GB     10.74GB

OR

podman machine list | head -n 2 | awk -F '\t' '{ print $2 }'


podman machine list | head -n 2 | awk -F '\t' '{ print $1 }'
NAME        VM TYPE     CREATED      LAST UP            CPUS        MEMORY      DISK SIZE
macvm*      qemu        2 hours ago  About an hour ago  1           2.147GB     10.74GB
```

**Additional information**

podman version: 3.4.2
OS: MacOS

comments.body:
Interested in opening a PR to fix?
@jwhonce have you been looking into similar fixes?

As a Report Template test case validates that the template populated include `\t`, then I suspect that the issue occurs when the result is stdout on macos 

Test case tested
```
package test

import (
	"bytes"
	report "github.com/containers/common/pkg/report"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"testing"
)

type machineReporter struct {
	Name     string
	Default  bool
	Created  string
	Running  bool
	LastUp   string
	VMType   string
	CPUs     uint64
	Memory   string
	DiskSize string
}

func TestTemplate_Parse(t *testing.T) {
	headers := report.Headers(machineReporter{}, map[string]string{
		"LastUp":   "LAST UP",
		"VmType":   "VM TYPE",
		"CPUs":     "CPUS",
		"Memory":   "MEMORY",
		"DiskSize": "DISK SIZE",
	})

	listFormat := "{{.Name}}\t{{.VMType}}\t{{.Created}}\t{{.LastUp}}\t{{.CPUs}}\t{{.Memory}}\t{{.DiskSize}}\n"
	row := report.NormalizeFormat(listFormat)
	format := report.EnforceRange(row)

	var buf bytes.Buffer
	tmpl, e := report.NewTemplate("TestTemplate").Parse(format)
	assert.NoError(t, e)

	// Check the headers content
	err := tmpl.Execute(&buf, headers)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "NAME\tVM TYPE\tCREATED\tLAST UP\tCPUS\tMEMORY\tDISK SIZE\n", buf.String())
	buf.Reset()

	// Check the machineReport
	machineReporter:= genMachineReporter()
	err = tmpl.Execute(&buf, machineReporter)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "macvm*\tqemu\t6 hours ago\t5 hours ago\t1\t2.147GB\t10.74GB\n", buf.String())
}

func genMachineReporter() ([]*machineReporter) {
	vms := make([]*machineReporter,0)
	vm := new(machineReporter)
	vm.Default = false
	vm.Running = false
	vm.Name = "macvm*"
	vm.LastUp = "5 hours ago"
	vm.Created = "6 hours ago"
	vm.VMType = "qemu"
	vm.CPUs = 1
	vm.Memory = "2.147GB"
	vm.DiskSize = "10.74GB"
	vms = append(vms, vm)
	return vms
}
```

The error occurs when we use the `Report Tab Writer` --> see:
```
	// Use now a Tabwriter
	w := tabwriter.NewWriter(&buf, 12, 2, 2, ' ', 0)
	err = tmpl.Execute(w, machineReporter)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	w.Flush()
	res := buf.String()
	assert.Equal(t, "macvm*\tqemu\t6 hours ago\t5 hours ago\t1\t2.147GB\t10.74GB\n", res)

--->
Error:      	Not equal: 
        	expected: "macvm*\tqemu\t6 hours ago\t5 hours ago\t1\t2.147GB\t10.74GB\n"
        	actual  : "macvm*      qemu        6 hours ago  5 hours ago  1           2.147GB     10.74GB\n"
```
The solution is to change the `TabWriter` created within the `machine list` [command](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/cmd/podman/machine/list.go#L127) 
```
w, err := report.NewWriterDefault(os.Stdout)
```

from
```
NewWriter(output, 12, 2, 2, ' ', 0) 
```

to
```
NewWriter(output, 0, 5, 0, '\t', 0)
```

WDYT ?

@jwhonce @rhatdan
I believe @jwhonce is looking into this one already, for another command (but they all use the same display code, so in theory it should be the same fix).
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@jwhonce Any progress?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@ashley-cui this  might be fixed, can you please take a look and verify.

Not fixed, but also not sure if this is a bug. The suggested fix breaks the formatting completely:
> from
> 
> ```
> NewWriter(output, 12, 2, 2, ' ', 0) 
> ```
> 
> to
> 
> ```
> NewWriter(output, 0, 5, 0, '\t', 0)
> ```
> 
![Screenshot from 2022-07-21 16-33-40](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/32910034/180310455-dd76c8f2-1b4a-4a89-ad20-902df217e875.png)

I'm not sure if we should honor the `\t`, as Docker also renders it as spaces. 

@rhatdan @jwhonce WDYT?
I think the request was for if the user specifies \t in their formatter, then we should use a <tab> rather then <spaces>
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@ashley-cui any progress?
Not at the moment
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Hello, can I try this one? And can you guide me to find the code in the source code?

> @SamirPS Sure.
> 
> The code is right here. https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/cmd/podman/machine/list.go

Do you know what function?

Hey I would like to work on this one
How do I test the changes
You got it. Not sure the best way to test this.

I don't think we should change the formatting, this is not specific to the machine ls commands, all commands that output formatted data work that way. 

On current main your first example seems to work fine when the format is specified by the user:
```
$ bin/podman machine list --format "{{.Name}}\t{{.VMType}}\t{{.Created}}\t{{.LastUp}}\t{{.CPUs}}\t{{.Memory}}\t{{.DiskSize}}" | awk -F '\t' '{ print $1,$5 }'
podman-machine-default* 1
```
OK, let's close it.  I prefer consistency and Paul mentioned a workaround above.
